metadata.title: Universe of Finance — Annual TPS Time-Series 2015-2025
metadata.description: Machine-readable time-series for all 24 categories covering annual average TPS and annual transaction counts from 2015 to 2025 (current/estimated).
metadata.methodology: Extracted from per-category data.json historic arrays. Gaps filled by interpolation using sector growth rates or back-extrapolation from known anchors. See confidence field per data point.
notes: 2025 values are estimates/projections from baseline scenarios where available, or extrapolated from 2024 at recent growth rates
Categories with null historic data have been interpolated where reasonable, marked confidence: 'interpolated'
CAGR calculated only where both endpoints have values

categories.name: Bank Transfers (ACH/RTP/RTGS)

2015.source: Back-extrapolated from 2018 ACI data (~35% CAGR for RTP)

categories.notes: Primarily real-time payment volume. Batch ACH adds ~106B more stable txns. RTGS wire adds ~0.4B high-value txns.

2015.source: Back-extrapolated from World Bank value data

categories.name: Fixed Income (Bond Markets)
categories.notes: Cash bonds only. Repo would add ~84M more trades/year. Historic data is almost entirely interpolated.

categories.name: Exchange-Traded Derivatives
categories.notes: Explosive growth driven by Indian options market (NSE). One of the best-documented categories.

categories.name: OTC Derivatives
categories.notes: Excludes FX derivatives (counted in forex category). Notional outstanding well-known; trade count is modeled.

categories.name: Foreign Exchange
categories.notes: Institutional trades only. Including retail FX (~10M/day) would raise TPS to ~150. BIS Triennial is the anchor.

categories.name: Interbank Settlement (RTGS)
categories.notes: 2024 historic is for 3 systems only. 2025 baseline covers 13 RTGS systems (1.58B txns). Earlier years undercount.

categories.name: Securities Settlement
categories.notes: Historic figures cover Euroclear only. 2025 figure includes DTCC, Clearstream, T2S -- methodological break.

categories.name: Centralised Crypto Exchanges
categories.notes: Wash trading makes all historic counts unreliable. 25% blended reduction applied to 2024. Crypto cycles dominate.

categories.name: DeFi (Decentralised Finance)
categories.notes: Full subset of blockchain-l1-l2. Transaction counts are rough estimates. Volume values are better documented.

categories.name: Stablecoins

2022.source: UST collapse year but USDT/USDC grew

categories.notes: Full subset of blockchain-l1-l2. Transfer value ($27.6T) is higher confidence than count. On-chain verifiable.

categories.name: L1 & L2 Blockchain Transactions
categories.notes: On-chain data is transparent but definitions vary. Solana vote/spam filtering applied for 2024-25.

categories.name: AI Agent Transactions
categories.notes: Brand new category. x402 protocol launched 2025. AP2 (Google) nascent. All numbers are pre-market.

categories.name: IoT & Machine-to-Machine Payments

2015.source: Estimated from toll collection + early smart meters

categories.notes: Definition boundary is fuzzy. Toll collection dominates volume. EV charging and smart meters growing fast.

categories.name: Tokenised Real-World Assets
categories.notes: Full subset of blockchain-l1-l2. TVL tracked by RWA.xyz; transaction counts are modeled. Growth from near-zero base.

categories.name: In-Game Microtransactions

2015.source: Back-extrapolated from Newzoo data (~$45B IAP revenue / ~$7 ATV)

categories.notes: Revenue-derived. ATV assumptions drive count uncertainty. Post-COVID normalization flattened growth.

categories.name: Tax & Government Payments
categories.notes: Government payments are reliable in value but transaction counts are almost never published.

global_totals.notes: Sum of all 24 categories. Contains overlaps (e.g., card-rail wallets, UPI in both bank-transfers and digital-wallets). Not deduplicated. Use OVERLAP_MATRIX.md for dedup.
